AMAT.headline: DRAM/HBM share of Semiconductor Systems jumped from 26% to 34% in one quarter, confirming HBM capital-intensity thesis; foundry/logic soft but services growing 15%.

shifts.trend: DRAM share of Semiconductor Systems: 26% (FY25) → 34% (Q1 FY26, +8pts); foundry/logic: 67% → 62% (-5pts)
shifts.note: HBM technology-transition spending reshaping WFE mix faster than expected

shifts.trend: Revenue +4% FY25 → -2% YoY Q1 FY26; SemiSys -8% YoY as foundry/logic weakness offset memory gain
shifts.note: Net total flat — memory offsetting logic; not a demand problem, a mix problem

shifts.trend: China $8.5B (-16%) FY25 → $2.1B Q1 FY26 (stable ~30% of revenue; annual run-rate ~$8.4B)
shifts.note: China revenue bottomed out after FY24→FY25 decline; no further deterioration signaled

shifts.trend: Gross margin 48.7% (FY25) → 49.0% (Q1 FY26, +0.2pts); operating margin hit by one-time $253M BIS legal settlement
shifts.note: Core GM healthy; the settlement is non-recurring

shifts.trend: $253M BIS legal settlement in Q1 FY26 (for 2015–2021 China export violations); no new enforcement actions cited
shifts.note: Historical tail, not an ongoing flow

AMAT.verdict: The HBM mix shift is faster than consensus modeled — WFE tools serving DRAM/packaging are the growth driver even as total revenue is flat. Clean thesis: buy the DRAM capex cycle via AMAT, not the foundry/logic recovery.

AMD.headline: Data Center growth re-accelerated to +57% in Q1 FY26 from +32% FY25; gross margin expanded 3pts and China optionality partially restored via MI325 licenses.

shifts.trend: Data Center +32% YoY (FY25 full year) → +57% YoY (Q1 FY26); DC share of total revenue 47% → 56%
shifts.note: MI350/EPYC driving the acceleration

shifts.trend: Non-GAAP gross margin ~50% (FY25) → 53% (Q1 FY26, +3pts) on favorable DC mix
FY25: $800M MI308 charge, $360M reversed; Q1 FY26: MI325 licenses granted with 25% US re-import tariff
shifts.note: China optionality partially restored — tariff adds friction but licenses issued

shifts.trend: Client+Gaming +51% FY25 → +23% Q1 FY26 (decelerating); DC +32% → +57% (accelerating); DC now dominant driver
R&D +39% and MG&A +41% in Q1 FY26 vs flat/modest in FY25 — heavy AI investment phase
shifts.note: OpenAI 6 GW deal (FY25) validating the spend cadence

AMD.verdict: Thesis intact and building — DC growth re-accelerated two quarters after the OpenAI 6 GW deal and margin expansion is tracking. China risk is neutralized at a tariff cost rather than a binary block.

AMKR.headline: Revenue inflected from +6% FY25 to +28% Q1 2026 across all four end markets; capex surged 3x to $2.5-3.0B as Arizona fab construction accelerates.

shifts.trend: Revenue +6.2% FY25 → +27.5% Q1 2026; communications +42%, computing +19%, automotive/industrial +28%, consumer +4%
shifts.note: All four end markets positive simultaneously for first time in several quarters

shifts.trend: Gross margin 14.0% (FY25) → 14.2% (Q1 2026, +2.3pts YoY); Vietnam Facility ramp inefficiencies abating
FY25 capex $904.6M → Q1 2026 $224.6M (vs $79.9M Q1 2025); FY26 full-year guided $2.5-3.0B (3x)
shifts.note: Arizona advanced-packaging fab (1.8M sq ft, manufacturing begins H1 2028) is the primary driver

shifts.trend: Apple 29.8%, Qualcomm 11.1% in FY25; top-10 = 72% — no change signaled in Q1 2026
shifts.note: Apple concentration remains the single largest risk

AMKR.verdict: Revenue inflection and capex commitment are both confirmed. Arizona is the optionality call on advanced packaging for HBM/2.5D — Apple concentration is the persistent tail risk.

AMZN.headline: AWS growth re-accelerated from +20% FY25 to +28% Q1 2026; RPO surged $120B in one quarter to $364B after OpenAI expanded to $100B; capex on pace for $170B+ in 2026.

shifts.trend: AWS revenue +20% YoY (FY25) → +28% YoY (Q1 2026); the acceleration is durable, not base-effect driven
AWS operating margin 35.4% (FY25) → 37.7% (Q1 2026, +2.3pts) even as capex soars
shifts.note: Scale benefits flowing through despite massive infrastructure investment

shifts.trend: Cash capex $128.3B (FY25 full year) → $43.2B (Q1 2026 alone); annualized $170B+
shifts.note: Both AWS infrastructure and fulfillment capex cited as increasing for 2026

shifts.trend: OpenAI AWS commitment: $38B existing → expanded by $100B over 8 years; $50B total Amazon investment in OpenAI
shifts.note: Q1 2026 single deal added ~$100B to RPO

shifts.trend: RPO $244B (Dec 2025) → $364B (Mar 2026); $120B expansion in one quarter signals demand substantially outpacing capacity

AMZN.verdict: AWS re-acceleration plus hyperscaler-scale OpenAI deal validates the thesis. The single risk is whether $170B+ capex generates returns — AWS margin expansion so far says yes.

ANET.headline: Revenue accelerated from +28.6% FY25 to +35.1% Q1 FY26; deferred revenue growing faster than recognized revenue; first margin compression from supply tightening in AI Ethernet components.

shifts.trend: Revenue +28.6% (FY25) → +35.1% (Q1 FY26); Cloud and AI Titans 48% of FY25 revenue and growing
Q1 FY26 filing explicitly names memory and silicon supply tightening as gross-margin headwind for the first time
shifts.note: ANET is procuring ahead of demand — inventory building

shifts.trend: Gross margin 64.1% (FY25) → 61.9% (Q1 FY26, -2.2pts) on supply-cost pressure and large-customer mix
shifts.note: First negative GM trend across these two reports

shifts.trend: Two end customers 26% and 16% of FY25 revenue; AI Titans and Cloud share rising toward 50%+
Deferred revenue $5.37B (FY25-end) → $6.20B (Mar 2026 quarter-end, +$830M in one quarter)
shifts.note: Order pace exceeding revenue recognition — strong forward visibility

ANET.verdict: Growth is accelerating and backlog continues to build. The first margin warning (supply tightness) is new risk but not thesis-breaking — it reflects demand exceeding supply, which is bullish for the networking cycle.

AVGO.headline: Semiconductor segment accelerated from +22% FY25 to +52% Q1 FY26 on AI ASIC demand; customer concentration tightened sharply (top 5: ~40% → ~50%, one distributor 32% → 42%).

shifts.trend: Semiconductor solutions +22% (FY25) → +52% (Q1 FY26) on custom AI accelerators (XPUs) and AI networking
shifts.note: Acceleration broad-based across custom silicon and networking

shifts.trend: Top 5 end customers ~40% of revenue (FY25) → ~50% (Q1 FY26); one semi distributor 32% → 42% — single hyperscaler XPU concentration building
Gross margin 68% in both periods (FY25 and Q1 FY26); semi GM gain offset by mix
Operating income $25.5B (40% margin) FY25 → growing in Q1 FY26 on semi scale; VMware fully absorbed, restructuring tail minimal

AVGO.verdict: AI ASIC acceleration is confirmed across back-to-back periods. Customer concentration tightening is the primary new risk — 42% one-distributor revenue implies single hyperscaler XPU exposure. Thesis intact, concentration premium needs to be monitored.

CAT.headline: Power & Energy accelerated from +12% FY25 to +22% Q1 2026; backlog continuing to grow sequentially; tariffs are the new quantified headwind ($700M Q2 2026, $2.2-2.4B full year).

shifts.trend: Power & Energy +12% (FY25) → +22% (Q1 2026); Power Generation +32% (FY25 annual) → continued strength Q1
shifts.note: Data center prime power demand consistent driver both periods

shifts.trend: Backlog $51.2B at YE25 (from $30.0B at YE24) → continued QoQ growth in Q1 2026; RPO backlog $19.3B not expected filled in 2026
Tariff costs new in Q1: $700M expected Q2 2026, $2.2-2.4B full-year 2026; $1.0B IEEPA charges incurred in Q1 (recovery not probable)
shifts.note: SCOTUS ruled IEEPA tariffs unauthorized but CAT has absorbed the cost

shifts.trend: Adjusted operating margin 18.0% Q1 2026 (vs 18.3% prior year) — tariffs partially offset by pricing + volume

CAT.verdict: Power Generation demand from data centers confirmed across both periods. Tariffs add a quantified headwind (~$2.4B FY26) but backlog growth suggests pricing power is absorbing it.

CDNS.headline: Revenue accelerated from +14% FY25 to +19% Q1 FY26; China growing +19% then +36% despite BIS overhang; backlog expanded to $8.0B; operating margin stable at 29%.

shifts.trend: Revenue +14% (FY25) → +19% (Q1 FY26); product & maintenance +21% Q1
China revenue +19% (FY25, $680M) → +36% (Q1 FY26, $189M); China 11% → 13% of mix
shifts.note: China outperforming despite BIS 50%-owned-affiliate rule and tariff noise

shifts.trend: Backlog $7.8B (YE25) → $8.0B (Mar 2026); 55% to recognize in next 12 months (up from 53%)
shifts.note: Backlog growing and near-term recognition mix improving

shifts.trend: Operating margin 28% (FY25) → 29% (Q1 FY26); recurring revenue 77% vs 82% prior as hardware mix grew

CDNS.verdict: EDA compounder accelerating. China exposure expanding rather than contracting despite BIS noise — a positive signal that the May 2025 is-informed letter and its July rescission are behind it. No thesis-changing findings.

CEG.headline: Calpine acquisition closed Jan 7, 2026, tripling scale to 55 GW and 23 GW gas; hyperscaler PPA pipeline deepened with Microsoft 20-yr TMI and Meta 20-yr Clinton PPAs locked.

shifts.trend: Scale jump: standalone nuclear utility → 55 GW combined power producer post-Calpine; adds 23 GW natural gas (21 GW net post-divestitures)
shifts.note: Qualitatively different company post-close — not comparable YoY

shifts.trend: Hyperscaler PPAs: Q3 (pre-close) had Calpine approvals pending; FY25 10-K adds $1B DOE TMI loan (Microsoft PPA backed) + Meta 20-yr Clinton PPA
shifts.note: Both largest US hyperscalers now locked in long-term nuclear-power agreements

shifts.trend: GAAP net income declining (nuclear PTC timing); adjusted EPS $3.04 Q3 vs $2.74 prior year — underlying improving
shifts.note: Calpine leverage will affect GAAP earnings near-term

shifts.trend: $1.0B DOE loan for Crane (TMI) restart; Calpine $22B purchase financed via ~50M shares + $4.5B cash; leverage increased meaningfully

CEG.verdict: Post-Calpine, CEG is structurally transformed. Hyperscaler nuclear PPA coverage is deepening (both Microsoft and Meta locked). The investing risk is Calpine leverage, not demand.

CLS.headline: CCS revenue re-accelerated from +42% FY25 to +76% Q1 FY26; Enterprise segment (next-gen AI compute) reversed -19% to +101% QoQ — new AI/ML program cycle starting; revolver upsized to $1.75B.

shifts.trend: CCS +42% (FY25) → +76% (Q1 FY26); HPS +81% (FY25) → +63% (Q1 FY26) still very strong
Enterprise end-market: -19% (FY25) → +101% (Q1 FY26) as next-gen hyperscaler AI/ML compute program ramps
shifts.note: The paused program from 2025 is now in full ramp — step-change in revenue

shifts.trend: Top 3 CCS customers 32%/14%/12% in FY25; top 10 = 79%; pattern likely similar in Q1 FY26
shifts.note: Hyperscaler concentration high and sticky

shifts.trend: FY26 capex guided ~$1B (6% of revenue, vs historical 1.5-2%); Q1 26 revolver upsized $750M → $1.75B to fund expansion
shifts.note: Thailand, US, Mexico, Japan expansions underway

shifts.trend: CCS segment margin 8.4% (Q4 FY25) → 8.6% (Q1 FY26); operating leverage building

CLS.verdict: The thesis is advancing — the Enterprise next-gen AI/ML ramp unlocked a new growth vector on top of the already-elevated HPS networking run rate. Financing upsized appropriately for the capacity build.

CMI.headline: Power Systems EBITDA margin expanded from 22.7% (FY25) to 29.5% (Q1 FY26); Power Generation growth accelerating; data center backlog still cited at 6-8 quarters.

shifts.trend: Power Generation +19% (FY25) → +28% (Q1 FY26); Power Systems segment +16% → +19%
Power Systems EBITDA margin 22.7% (FY25) → 29.5% (Q1 FY26, +6.8pts) on volume leverage, pricing, and lower net tariff costs
shifts.note: Record Q1 margin driven by data-center-driven mix

shifts.trend: 'Strong demand for data center products extending out six to eight quarters' (FY25) — same language repeated in Q1 FY26 update
shifts.note: No easing in the demand horizon

shifts.trend: Distribution segment EBITDA +$68M in Q1 (data center power generation demand); China added as new Q1 growth driver alongside North America
PACCAR 13% and top 4 trucking OEMs 31% of sales — truck market still weak H1 26, offsetting DC strength

CMI.verdict: Power Generation thesis intact and margins expanding faster than modeled. The 6-8 quarter data center demand horizon (stated twice) confirms durable visibility. Weak truck market is the offset but Power Systems is the stock driver.

COHR.headline: D&C segment revenue accelerated from +34% to +41% and segment profit from +44% to +49%; gross margin expanded sequentially; 'scale across' AI interconnect named as a new demand category in Q3.

shifts.trend: Total revenue +17% (Q2 FY26) → +21% (Q3 FY26); D&C +34% → +41%; D&C 9-month revenue $3.66B +34% YoY
Gross margin +140bps YoY (Q2) → +243bps YoY (Q3); Q3 consolidated GM 38% vs 37% Q2
shifts.note: Yield improvements and portfolio narrowing (divestitures) driving margin expansion

shifts.trend: D&C segment profit +44% (Q2) → +49% (Q3); 9-month segment profit $923M +39%
shifts.note: Margin leverage building within the D&C segment

shifts.trend: Q3 first explicit naming of 'scale across' (inter-DC AI cluster interconnect) alongside DCI and traditional telecom as demand categories
shifts.note: New demand vocabulary = new addressable market emerging

shifts.trend: Portfolio narrowed: aerospace divested Sep 25, Munich divested Jan 26 — COHR becoming an AI optics pure-play

COHR.verdict: Both acceleration and margin expansion confirm the AI optics demand surge. The 'scale across' addition in Q3 is thesis-additive: a new inter-DC AI demand vector not previously quantified. Story intact and strengthening.

CRM.headline: Revenue stable at +9%→+10%; Agentforce 360 Platform +19% is the only double-digit growth segment; RPO recovered from $59.5B (Q3) to $72.4B (year-end) after Informatica close.

shifts.trend: Revenue +9% (Q3 FY26) → +10% (FY26 full year); subscription +10% both periods — consistent but not accelerating
Agentforce 360 Platform/Slack/Other +19% (Q3 FY26) — only double-digit segment; captures AI/Data Cloud consumption revenue
shifts.note: This is the leading indicator to watch for AI uplift

shifts.trend: Total RPO $59.5B (Oct 31 2025, declining from $63.4B at Jan 25) → $72.4B (Jan 31 2026, recovering); cRPO +16% year-end
shifts.note: Informatica close (Nov 2025) added to RPO; year-end backlog healthy

shifts.trend: Repurchased $12.7B (50M shares) + $1.6B dividends in FY26; operating cash flow $15.0B +15%
shifts.note: Capital returns well-funded from operations

shifts.trend: Attrition ~8% YoY unchanged; core ITSM/Sales/Service retention solid

CRM.verdict: CRM is a stable compounder at 10% growth — not an acceleration story. Agentforce adoption is the AI call option, but its contribution is still small. Informatica integration is the medium-term execution risk.

CRWV.headline: RPO surged from $60.7B to $98.8B in one quarter; Microsoft concentration fell from 67% to 45% as Meta emerged at 20%; revenue run-rate implies $8B+ annualized.

shifts.trend: Revenue $5.1B FY25 (full year) → $2.08B Q1 2026 ($8.3B annualized run-rate, +63% acceleration vs FY25 pace)
Microsoft share: 67% (FY25) → 45% (Q1 2026); Meta emerges at 20% — structural diversification improving
RPO $60.7B (Dec 2025) → $98.8B (Mar 2026); 36% recognized in next 24 months, 39% in months 25-48
shifts.note: $38B RPO expansion in one quarter

shifts.trend: Net loss $1.2B (FY25) → $740M (Q1 2026 alone); accumulated deficit $3.4B; capital-intensive model
shifts.note: Operating leverage still negative — watching for margin inflection

shifts.trend: 850 MW active (FY25) with 3.1 GW contracted; deferred revenue $7.5B at Mar 31 2026
shifts.note: Power siting is the binding constraint on growth pace

CRWV.verdict: Customer diversification (Microsoft 67%→45%, Meta 20%) is materially de-risking the story. RPO explosion to $98.8B validates the hyperscaler GPU-cloud demand. Leverage and power siting are the two execution risks.

CSCO.headline: Networking re-accelerated from +15% to +21% on AI Infrastructure; Silicon One purchase commitments +10% as hyperscaler capacity build continues; Security -4% on Splunk cloud shift.

shifts.trend: Total revenue +8% (Q1 FY26) → +10% (Q2 FY26); Networking +15% (+$1.0B) → +21% (+$1.4B); 1H FY26 Networking +18% (+$2.5B)
Inventory +7% and purchase commitments +10% vs FYE 2025 (Q1); ongoing Silicon One capacity commitments for hyperscalers cited in both quarters
shifts.note: CSCO is pre-buying to secure Silicon One capacity for AI Infrastructure orders

shifts.trend: Security -4% (Q2) on Splunk shift from on-prem license to cloud subscription; trend expected to continue 2H FY26
Service Provider and Cloud customer market led product growth in both Americas and EMEA in both quarters; AI Infrastructure Ethernet cited as primary driver
Product gross margin -0.6pts (Q1) → +0.2pts (Q2) on productivity offsetting mix; diluted EPS +31% Q2

CSCO.verdict: Networking acceleration from 15% to 21% across two quarters is the key signal — AI Infrastructure Ethernet is the durable demand driver. Silicon One inventory build de-risks near-term supply. Security Splunk transition is known and manageable.

ETN.headline: Electrical Americas backlog expanded from $13.2B (YE25) to $14.5B (Q1 26, +44% YoY) with orders +42% organic; margins compressed -480bps on commodity inflation — the main new concern.

shifts.trend: Electrical Americas backlog $13.2B (YE25, +31% YoY) → $14.5B (Q1 26, +44% YoY, +32% organic); book-to-bill 1.2x both periods
Electrical Americas organic orders +42% (Q1 26); Electrical Global backlog +73% YoY ($3.16B)
shifts.note: Order pace accelerating despite a strong FY25 baseline

shifts.trend: Electrical Americas operating margin Q1 26: 25.6% vs 30.0% prior year (-480bps from commodity inflation + tariffs, only partially offset by pricing)
shifts.note: First meaningful margin contraction vs the improving trend in FY25

shifts.trend: Boyd Thermal $9.5B acquisition (signed Nov 25, closes 2026) adds liquid cooling + thermal solutions — expanding into DC cooling

ETN.verdict: Backlog growth acceleration validates the data-center power thesis. The 480bps margin compression is the new risk to watch — commodity inflation is passing through orders but not yet fully into pricing. Boyd Thermal acquisition extends the thesis into liquid cooling.

FN.headline: Revenue accelerated from +35.9% to +39.3%; DCI (inter-datacenter AI interconnect) grew +90.4% YoY and was $197M in Q3 — now clearly the fastest-growing optical segment.

shifts.trend: Revenue +35.9% (Q2 FY26) → +39.3% (Q3 FY26); 9-month revenue +32.5% YoY
DCI revenue $142.2M (Q2) → $196.9M (Q3, +90.4% YoY) — fastest-growing line; Telecom $412.2M → $431.4M (+42.5%)
shifts.note: DCI now the key AI-demand driver; inter-DC fabric spending is new and expanding

shifts.trend: Customers >10%: 2 (48.5% combined) Q2 FY25 → 4 (59.1% combined) Q3 FY26 — concentration tightening rapidly
shifts.note: Likely large hyperscalers ordering direct

shifts.trend: Non-optical (HPC + automotive) +61.1% (Q2) → +51.7% (Q3) still very strong; HPC $85.6M → $106.7M
shifts.note: HPC is a new AI-adjacent growth vector in the non-optical bucket

shifts.trend: Gross margin 12.2% (Q2) → 11.9% (Q3); operating income +52.1% on volume leverage

FN.verdict: DCI hyper-growth (+90% YoY in Q3) is the key thesis-confirming signal — inter-datacenter AI cluster interconnect is a new and expanding demand category. Customer concentration tightening is worth monitoring but reflects order concentration, not churn risk.

GEV.headline: RPO reached $163.3B (+$13B in Q1 alone); heavy-duty gas turbine order pace maintained; Power segment EBITDA margin expanded from 14.7% to 16.3%.

shifts.trend: Total RPO $150.2B (YE25) → $163.3B (Q1 26, +9% QoQ); Power RPO $94.4B → $99.7B (+5% QoQ, +30% YoY)
shifts.note: RPO compounding — supply lead-times stretching backlog further out

shifts.trend: Heavy-Duty Gas Turbine orders: 68 (FY24) → 110 (FY25); Q1 26: 28 HD + 12 HA = ~40 quarterly run-rate annualizing to 160+ units
Power segment EBITDA margin 14.7% (FY25) → 16.3% (Q1 FY26, +1.6pts) on pricing + volume leverage
Revenue +9% (FY25) → +16% (Q1 FY26); adjusted EBITDA $3.2B (FY25) → Q1 alone $896M on track for $3.6B+

GEV.verdict: Gas turbine order momentum sustained and RPO compounding. Margin expansion confirms pricing power is flowing through after years of backlog-building. GEV is one of the clearest bottleneck-plays in the power layer.

GNRC.headline: Total revenue inflected from -2% (FY25) to +12% (Q1 FY26); C&I segment accelerated from +4.9% to +28% on data-center-driven generator demand; margins improved 120bps.

shifts.note: Data-center backup-power demand driving the inflection

shifts.trend: Adjusted EBITDA margin 17.0% (FY25) → 18.2% (Q1 FY26, +120bps); C&I margin 11.4% → 13.0%
shifts.note: Mix shift toward higher-margin C&I business improving blended margins

shifts.trend: Residential +1% Q1 — stable after -6.8% FY25 on quiet outage season; home standby not the growth story anymore
Large-megawatt diesel gensets launched in 2025 (FY25 10-K) now generating C&I order flow; Q1 mentions data center, industrial distributor, and rental channels as combined tailwinds
shifts.note: New product directly targeting DC backup power market

GNRC.verdict: C&I inflection to +28% with margin expansion confirms the data-center generator thesis. Residential weakness is absorbed. The large-MW diesel genset launch in 2025 is bearing fruit — this is a product-cycle inflection, not just a market-cycle inflection.

GOOGL.headline: Google Cloud re-accelerated from +36% (FY25) to +63% (Q1 2026) with margin tripling to 33%; RPO surged $225B in one quarter to $467.6B; 'multi-gigawatt' TPU supply agreements disclosed.

shifts.trend: Google Cloud revenue +36% (FY25) → +63% (Q1 2026); Google Cloud op income $13.9B (FY25) → $6.6B Q1 alone (annualized $26B+)
shifts.note: Fastest acceleration of any hyperscaler cloud segment across this window

shifts.trend: Cloud operating margin 23.7% (FY25) → 33% (Q1 2026, +9.3pts); scale benefits outpacing infra cost growth
Capex $91.4B (FY25) → $35.7B Q1 2026 alone; 2026 expected to 'significantly increase' vs $91B
RPO $242.8B (Dec 2025) → $467.6B (Mar 2026, +$225B); $462.3B is Google Cloud; multi-gigawatt TPU supply agreements revenue starting late 2026
Acquired Wiz ($32B) and Intersect data-center/energy ($4.8B) — M&A accelerating across AI security and infrastructure

GOOGL.verdict: Google Cloud is the most compelling hyperscaler re-acceleration story: +63% growth, 33% margin, and $467B RPO. The multi-gigawatt TPU supply agreements suggest GOOGL is competing with NVDA at the AI hardware level. Capex risk is real but margin expansion reassures.

HPE.headline: Juniper contributed first full quarter in Q1 FY26 — Networking +151.5%; Cloud & AI servers -2.7% on GPU pricing pressure; ARR +63% FY25 signals aaS shift is structural.

shifts.trend: Networking +51.1% (FY25, partial Juniper) → +151.5% (Q1 FY26, first full quarter); GM lifted +670bps by Juniper mix
shifts.note: Partially artificial from timing — 7 months FY25 vs 3 months prior year

shifts.trend: Cloud & AI (formerly Server) -2.7% Q1 FY26 — AI server growth offset by GPU-heavy mix negative ASP pressure
shifts.note: GPU-heavy configurations have lower ASPs relative to prior server mix

shifts.trend: Revenue +13.8% (FY25, partial Juniper) → +18.4% (Q1 FY26, full Juniper); underlying organic harder to parse
ARR $3.15B +63% (FY25) as Networking subscription mix grows; non-GAAP operating margin +280bps in Q1 FY26
GAAP operating loss $437M (FY25) from $1.6B goodwill impairment + $458M acquisition costs; Juniper integration ongoing $123M/qtr
shifts.note: GAAP metrics mask operational improvement; watch non-GAAP operating margin

HPE.verdict: Juniper integration is the thesis — networking mix lifts margins and ARR. Cloud & AI server weakness is new noise, but it is GPU-mix-related (a demand quality problem, not a demand problem). Integration costs are the known headwind.

HUBB.headline: Revenue accelerated from +3.8% FY25 to +11% Q1 FY26; both segments organic growth improved — Electrical Solutions from 7.2% to 10.6%, Utility Solutions from 1.1% to 6.8%.

shifts.trend: Total organic revenue +3.3% (FY25) → +8.2% (Q1 FY26); Electrical Solutions organic +7.2% → +10.6%; Utility Solutions organic +1.1% → +6.8%
Electrical Solutions (datacenter vertical) organic +10.6% Q1 FY26, margin 16.4% (-30bps) on commodity inflation; backlog $2.16B at YE25 pointing to 2026 delivery
Adjusted operating margin 22.5% (FY25) → Electrical Solutions margin 16.4% Q1 (-30bps YoY); overall margin broadly stable
shifts.note: Commodity and tariff pressures the marginal headwind

shifts.trend: Firm backlog $2.16B at YE25 substantially all to ship in 2026 — revenue conversion visibility is high

HUBB.verdict: Broad-based acceleration across both segments confirms that grid infrastructure and datacenter electrical spending are both supporting growth. Not priced as an AI infrastructure play — potentially underappreciated.

INTC.headline: DCAI re-accelerated to +22% in Q1 on supply-constrained demand pricing; Foundry external revenue remains negligible ($174M); 14A pause risk still disclosed; supply constraints to persist through H1 2026.

shifts.trend: DCAI +5.6% (FY25) → +22% (Q1 2026) on 27% higher ASPs from premium mix + demand-based pricing; volume -5% due to supply constraints
'Market demand exceeded available product supply' Q1 2026 — supply constraints (substrates, memory, components) cited to persist H1 2026
shifts.note: Demand exceeds supply → favorable for ASPs

shifts.trend: Intel Foundry external revenue $307M (FY25) → $174M (Q1 2026, Altera now external); Foundry op loss $2.4B Q1 vs $2.3B Q1 25
shifts.note: Foundry turnaround showing no progress; external revenue declining

shifts.trend: Intel 18A ramped to high-volume production (FY25); 14A pause risk still disclosed if no significant external customer secured
shifts.note: Foundry thesis remains binary

shifts.trend: $4.1B Q1 2026 restructuring charges; GAAP net loss $3.7B; $20B CHIPS Act equity escrow (159M shares) releasing slowly (3M released to date)

INTC.verdict: DCAI supply-constrained demand is thesis-supportive for near-term pricing. Foundry is the binary question — 14A viability depends on external customer win. Intel is two separate bets: a constrained-supply CPU server story and a binary foundry turnaround.

KLAC.headline: Revenue accelerated from +7% to +11%; Korea (memory) jumped +80% YoY in Q3 while China fell to 24% of mix; DRAM/HBM remains the dominant growth driver across both quarters.

shifts.trend: Revenue +7% (Q2 FY26) → +11% (Q3 FY26); memory DRAM/HBM consistently cited as primary driver
Korea revenue: $681M +80% YoY (Q3) vs +34% (Q2) — SK Hynix / Samsung HBM capex stepping up
China 30.2% of revenue (-9% YoY) in Q2 → 24% (-modest growth) Q3; nine-month China revenue flat at $3.09B on export controls
shifts.note: China share declining as Korea and North America dominate growth

shifts.trend: Gross margin 61.4% (Q2) → 61.1% (Q3, -0.3pts) from escalating DRAM chip costs in image computers; flagged as transitory
$548M repurchases + $250M dividends Q2; $626M repurchases + $249M dividends Q3; $7B added to buyback authorization

KLAC.verdict: DRAM/HBM investment thesis confirmed and accelerating — Korea +80% is the clearest indicator that SK Hynix and Samsung are ramping HBM capex hard. China drag is persistent but quantified. Thesis intact.

LEU.headline: Fluor EPC contract signed for Piketon LEU+HALEU expansion; backlog grew $100M to $3.9B; SWU pricing power intact at $200 spot vs $34 historical low.

shifts.trend: SWU spot price $200 (both periods, stable); LEU backlog $2.9B → $3.1B (+$200M); total backlog $3.8B → $3.9B
Revenue $76.7M Q1 FY26 (+5%); SWU volume -47% but ASP +52% — volume timing driven, pricing power confirmed
FY25: $900M HALEU task order + Piketon expansion announced; Q1 FY26: Fluor Federal Services EPC contract signed ($24M cancellation fee) — execution advancing
shifts.note: Milestone: moved from award to contract execution on HALEU commercial-scale plant

shifts.trend: Only US company with NRC license for active enrichment to 20% HALEU; produced/delivered 900 kg HALEU in prior DOE contract

LEU.verdict: LEU is a pre-revenue nuclear infrastructure story. Fluor EPC contract signature is the key de-risking milestone this period. SWU spot at $200 (vs $34 historical low) validates the pricing thesis. Scale is limited — this is a high-conviction niche, not a liquidity play.

LITE.headline: Revenue accelerated from +65.5% to +90.1%; gross margin expanded 1,130bps then 1,540bps YoY as 200G-lane InP laser mix lifts ASPs; 9-month revenue already $2.01B (+72.4%).

shifts.trend: Revenue +65.5% (Q2 FY26) → +90.1% (Q3 FY26); 9-month $2.01B +72.4% YoY; Components +77.3%, Systems +121.1% in Q3
Gross margin +1,130bps YoY (Q2) → +1,540bps YoY (Q3); Q3 GM 44.2% vs 29.6% year-ago — structural improvement
shifts.note: Supply allocation and 200G lane ASP mix are compounding

shifts.trend: Cloud transceiver growth: +$137M in Q3 on volume; 200G lane-speed mix lifting ASPs — confirms LITE as the InP/EML supplier for 1.6T
OCS (optical circuit switches): >$10M Q2 → contributing in Q3 — new AI switching product ramping
Q3 commentary: supply allocation and manufacturing expansion underway — demand exceeding current supply

LITE.verdict: The strongest acceleration story in the optics layer. +90% revenue with +1,540bps margin expansion in Q3 suggests LITE is in an early-cycle supply-constrained demand phase analogous to memory in 2023. If 200G lane capacity builds without over-shoot, margin can expand further.

LRCX.headline: Revenue grew +9% sequentially in Q3; DRAM/memory mix rose from 34% to 39% of systems revenue as HBM investment accelerates; China held at 34-35% but Korea jumped from 20% to 23%.

shifts.trend: Revenue flat QoQ (Q2, $5.34B) → +9% QoQ (Q3, $5.84B); 9-month revenue $16.51B vs $13.26B (+24% YoY)
Memory mix in systems: 34% (Q2) → 39% (Q3, +5pts) on strengthened DRAM and NVM investment; Foundry fell 59% → 54% on lower mature node spend
shifts.note: HBM capital cycle re-weighting LRCX toward DRAM tools

shifts.trend: China 35% (Q2) → 34% (Q3) of revenue; nine-month China trajectory stable despite export controls
shifts.note: China not deteriorating further — legacy node demand supporting flat share

shifts.trend: Korea 20% (Q2) → 23% (Q3) of revenue on memory capex from SK Hynix/Samsung; Taiwan 23% both quarters
Gross margin 49.6% (Q2) → 49.8% (Q3, +0.2pts) on better factory efficiencies; customer-support revenue $1.99B → $2.11B (+6%) on growing installed base
Q3 capital returns $1.16B repurchases + $326M dividends + $751M debt paydown; cash dropped to $4.77B from $6.20B
shifts.note: Aggressive return of capital while growing capex — confidence signal

LRCX.verdict: DRAM/HBM mix shift from 34% to 39% of systems revenue in one quarter is the key data point — LRCX tools are the direct play on HBM capacity expansion. China is stable-to-declining (managed), Korea is growing. Thesis intact.

META.headline: Revenue re-accelerated from +22% FY25 to +33% Q1 2026; 2026 capex guidance raised from $115-135B to $125-145B; ad impressions growth accelerated to 19% and price/ad to +12%.

shifts.trend: Revenue +22% (FY25) → +33% (Q1 2026); ad impressions +12% (FY25) → +19% (Q1 2026); price per ad +9% → +12%
shifts.note: Both volume and pricing components accelerating

shifts.trend: Capex $72.2B (FY25) → $19.8B Q1 2026; FY26 guide RAISED from $115-135B to $125-145B (implies +57-82% vs FY25)
shifts.note: Second consecutive guide raise — capex commitment strengthening

shifts.trend: Non-cancelable commitments $237.7B at Mar 31 (mostly cloud capacity + servers + data centers); $42.8B within 12 months
Reality Labs operating loss $4.03B Q1 ($16B annualized run-rate); FY26 full-year expected similar to FY25 $19.2B
shifts.note: RL burn rate: the largest structural drag on FCF, but core ad business absorbs it

META.verdict: Revenue acceleration with capex raise confirms META is investing from a position of strength, not desperation. Both ad metrics accelerating simultaneously is rare and thesis-positive. RL losses are the known and tolerable drag.

MOD.headline: Climate Solutions data center revenue surged from +$67M to +$130M YoY uplift; Gentherm RMT announced to create pure-play cooling company; margin drag from capacity ramp persisting.

shifts.trend: Climate Solutions total: +24% (Q2) → +51% (Q3); datacenter uplift $67.4M Q2 → $130.0M Q3; 9M DC product sales up $221.7M YoY
Q2 segment revenue $454.4M → Q3 $544.6M — sequential lift driven by DC product growth (hyperscale + colo, NA + Europe)
Climate Solutions operating margin 13.7% (Q2) → 15.3% (Q3); both below pre-ramp 17.6% — DC manufacturing capacity inefficiencies still materializing
shifts.note: Progress on margin recovery but not yet restored

shifts.trend: Jan 2026: Reverse Morris Trust announced to spin Performance Technologies, merge with Gentherm; MOD retains Climate Solutions — becoming a pure-play DC cooling + commercial HVAC company
shifts.note: Transaction value ~$1B; expected to close end of CY26

MOD.verdict: Revenue growth is accelerating fast in the DC cooling segment. The RMT creates a pure-play Climate Solutions company, which should remove the industrial conglomerate discount. Margin recovery is the execution risk — ramp inefficiencies need to normalize.

MRVL.headline: Data Center accelerated from +38% to +46% on full-year basis; Distributor A concentration 38%→37% (flat); automotive sold to Infineon for $2.5B; Celestial AI acquired for photonic interconnects.

shifts.trend: Data Center +38% (Q3 FY26) → +46% (FY26 full year); DC 73% → 74% of revenue
Gross margin 51.6% (Q3 FY26) → 51.0% (FY26 full year); structural improvement vs 23%/41% in prior year (which included restructuring impairments)
shifts.note: Underlying GM improvement significant; comparison distorted by FY25 write-downs

shifts.trend: Communications recovery: +31% FY26 as enterprise ethernet rebounds from cyclical trough
Portfolio pivot: automotive ethernet sold to Infineon ($2.5B, Aug 2025); Celestial AI acquired (~$1.3B, Feb 2026) for photonic interconnects
shifts.note: Asset swap: commoditizing auto ethernet out, AI-era photonic fabric in

shifts.trend: Distributor A 38% (Q3 FY26) → 37% (FY26) — roughly stable; top-customer direct 11% → 14%

MRVL.verdict: MRVL is executing the portfolio transformation: exiting low-value auto and buying into AI photonics. DC growth acceleration plus the Celestial AI photonic-fabric bet make this a high-conviction ASIC + optics crossover story. The concentration in one distributor remains the risk.

MSFT.headline: Azure slightly accelerated from +39% to +40%; commercial RPO held at $625-627B; H2 capex pace accelerating with Q3 alone at $30.9B vs Q2 at $29.9B.

shifts.trend: Azure +39% (Q2 FY26) → +40% (Q3 FY26); Microsoft Cloud +26% → +29%
Commercial RPO $625B (Jan 2026) → $627B (Mar 2026) — stable at sky-high levels; ~25% to recognize next 12 months
shifts.note: Multi-year AI commitments locking in the next several years of Cloud revenue

shifts.trend: H1 FY26 capex $49.3B; Q3 alone $30.9B (pace accelerating); 9M total $80.1B vs $40.9B prior year (+96%)
Cloud gross margin 67% Q2 → slightly recovering in Q3 on AI infrastructure amortization; operating income growing with revenue

MSFT.verdict: Azure consistently above 39-40% growth and $625B+ RPO provides multi-year revenue visibility. The capex run-rate doubling YoY ($80.1B vs $40.9B 9M) is the thesis risk — returns need to materialize. So far cloud margin is holding.

MU.headline: Two-quarter beat-and-raise cadence accelerating — Q2 revenue beat guide by 28%, EPS by 45%; Q3 guide implies further +40% QoQ.

shifts.note: QoQ growth re-accelerated from +21% to +75% and is guided to +40% again
shifts.trend: Revenue $13.6B → $23.9B actual → $33.5B guide (YoY: +57% → +196% → implied +250%+)

shifts.note: Pricing power compounding — AI demand outpaces industry supply
shifts.trend: Non-GAAP GM 56.8% → 74.9% → ~81% guide (≈ +24 pts of expansion in two quarters)

shifts.note: Beat magnitude widening, not narrowing — credibility on the raised Q3 guide is high
shifts.trend: Q2 revenue guided $18.7B → printed $23.9B (+28% beat); Q3 now guided to $33.5B (+40% QoQ)

shifts.note: Same constraint language for two consecutive quarters; tightness is persistent, not transient
shifts.trend: 'Supply allocation decisions may impact certain customers' (Q1) → 'Industry supply allocation decisions continue' (Q2) — no easing language added

shifts.note: HBM hyperscaler reliance increasing — single-customer dependency to monitor
shifts.trend: One customer reached 13% of six-month revenue (Q2 disclosure) — primarily CMBU (HBM/hyperscale)

shifts.note: Singapore HBM advanced-packaging fab broke ground; capacity additions from CY2027
shifts.trend: FY26 capex ~$20B (Q1 guide); Q1 capex $5.39B + Q2 capex $5.0B = $10.4B run-rate H1 — on pace for $20B+

shifts.note: First meaningful dividend hike in years; mgmt signaling cycle durability
shifts.trend: Dividend $0.115/share (Q1) → $0.15/share (Q2, +30%)

MU.verdict: Thesis intact and strengthening — beat-and-raise cadence is widening, gross-margin expansion is compounding, and guidance credibility is high. The only emerging risk is HBM customer concentration (one customer at 13% of revenue). Supply tightness language has not eased — bullish for memory pricing through CY2026.

MXL.headline: Revenue accelerated from +30% FY25 to +43% Q1 2026; infrastructure (optical DSPs) consistent double-digit grower; customer concentration deconcentrating (top customer 20%→13%).

shifts.trend: Revenue +30% (FY25) → +43% (Q1 2026); Infrastructure +$34.3M (FY25) → +$36.3M (Q1 2026) consistently strong
Top customer >20% historically → 13% (Q1 2026); top 10 = 56% (vs prior higher concentrations)
shifts.note: Deconcentration is positive — less single-customer risk

shifts.trend: Infrastructure (optical DSPs for 400G→800G→1.6T switch transition) cited as primary growth driver in both periods
shifts.note: Switch silicon interconnect roadmap locks in multi-year optical DSP upgrade cycle

shifts.trend: Gross margin 57% +3pts (FY25) from mix improvement and lower intangible amortization; Q1 2026 trajectory stable

MXL.verdict: Infrastructure optical DSP ramp is the thesis and it is tracking. Revenue acceleration to 43% with customer deconcentration are both positive signals. MXL is the smallest and most volatile name in the optics/networking layer — but the 1.6T roadmap provides a durable upgrade cycle.

NOW.headline: Revenue stable at 21-22% growth with consistent RPO and cRPO; Now Assist AI deeply embedded but no measurable acceleration in revenue yet; compounder characteristics intact.

shifts.trend: Revenue +21% (FY25) → +22% (Q1 2026); subscription +21% → +22% — consistent but not accelerating
Total RPO +27% (FY25, $28.2B) → +25% (Q1 2026, $27.7B); cRPO +25% → +23% — marginal deceleration, normal seasonality
Now Assist AI across ITSM/CSM/HRSD/FSM in both periods; self-hosted upfront $492M (FY25) → $138M Q1 (slight decline)
shifts.note: AI narrative stable but not yet revenue-accelerating

shifts.trend: Subscription gross margin consistently above 80%; operating leverage consistent across both periods

NOW.verdict: Consistent 22% compounder. No evidence of Now Assist inflection in revenue yet — the AI story is embedded in retention and cross-sell, not a standalone acceleration. Thesis unchanged: steady-state compounder with AI option value.

NVDA.headline: DC growth holding ~+66–68% YoY; customer concentration formally disclosed at 4-way >10%; China is now effectively zero, but supply/energy commentary unchanged.

shifts.note: Slight re-acceleration on a full-year basis after Blackwell ramp
shifts.trend: DC YoY +66% (Q3 FY26) → +68% (FY26 full year); Networking +162% on GB200/GB300 NVLink ramp

shifts.note: Hyperscaler dependence newly visible — diligence risk-additive
shifts.trend: Q3 disclosed 4 direct customers each >10% of revenue (22/15/13/11%); 10-K did not previously break this out

shifts.note: China optionality fully impaired across both filings
shifts.trend: Q3 China $3.0B vs $8.1B prior-year Q3; H20 effectively zero by FY26 after $4.5B Q1 inventory charge; Feb 2026 H200 license carries 25% US import tariff
Non-GAAP GM 73.4% (Q3) → 73.5% guide (Q1 FY27); compressed YoY on Blackwell full-system mix but holding within target

shifts.note: Quote nearly identical across the two filings
shifts.trend: Both filings cite data-center / energy / capital availability as the gating constraint on customer deployments — no easing language added

shifts.note: Vendor-financing footprint growing — watch FCF translation
shifts.trend: FY26 10-K disclosed $17.5B in private/infra investments plus $3.5B land/power/shell guarantees to early-stage AI customers; Q3 added $7.5B new DC lease obligations FY26 Q4–FY30

NVDA.verdict: Story still about supply-constrained AI capex. The new disclosures (4-way customer concentration, vendor-financing footprint) are risk-additive but don't break the thesis; China optionality is now fully impaired and should be modeled at zero.

NVT.headline: Revenue acceleration from +29.5% to +53.5%; Systems Protection organic growth jumped from 17% to 50.1% as data-center infrastructure contribution went from ~16pts to ~46pts of organic growth.

shifts.trend: Total revenue +29.5% FY25 (organic 12.6%) → +53.5% Q1 FY26 (organic 34.4%); most of acceleration is organic, not M&A
Systems Protection: +42.2% FY25 → +76.1% Q1 FY26; organic growth contribution from infrastructure ~16pts (FY25) → ~46pts (Q1 FY26)
shifts.note: DC infrastructure is now the dominant organic growth driver

shifts.trend: Systems Protection margin 22.5% (FY25) → 22.7% (Q1 FY26, +22bps) — holding despite tariff and raw material headwinds
shifts.note: Tariffs compressed gross margin 290bps but volume leverage offset

shifts.trend: Avail Infrastructure Solutions acquired May 2025 ($1.0B); Thermal Management divested for $1.03B — portfolio being sharpened toward DC infrastructure

NVT.verdict: The organic acceleration from 12.6% to 34.4% is the most compelling data point in this report set — it means the DC infrastructure surge is not just acquisition math. NVT looks like a cooling/power sleeper with the DC thesis confirmed in back-to-back periods.

OKLO.headline: Meta 1.2 GW prepayment agreement signed Jan 2026; DOE Aurora Safety Design approved Feb 2026; order book still at ~18,100 MWe; cash runway extended to $1.5B ATM.

shifts.trend: Order book ~18,100 MWe (Q3 FY25) → Meta 1.2 GW added Jan 2026 (not yet counted in order book as of FY25 10-K)
shifts.note: New demand structure: first prepayment agreement secured, changing the financial model

shifts.trend: Aurora Nuclear Safety Design Agreement approved by DOE Feb 2026; first commercial Aurora powerhouse deployment target 2027
shifts.note: Key regulatory de-risking milestone crossed in this window

shifts.trend: 9M cash used in operations $48.7M (Q3 FY25); FY25 opex $139.3M (+164%); $1.5B ATM program raised Dec 25–Jan 26; cash runway extended
Switch 12 GW master agreement + Meta 1.2 GW prepayment — two hyperscaler commitments now anchor the order book

OKLO.verdict: The two key milestones in this window — Meta prepayment and DOE Safety Design approval — are the inflection points the thesis depends on. Both confirmed. Oklo remains pre-commercial; execution risk is the first Aurora deployment in 2027.

ORCL.headline: OCI revenue accelerated from +68% to +84%; RPO held at $523-553B; capex tripled YoY with Q3 alone at $18.7B as the OpenAI cluster ramp drove sequential acceleration.

shifts.trend: OCI (Cloud infrastructure) +68% (Q2 FY26) → +84% (Q3 FY26); total revenue +14% → +22%
H1 FY26 capex $20.5B → 9M $39.2B (Q3 alone ~$18.7B); vs $12.1B 9M FY25 (3.2x increase); expected to continue 'next few fiscal years'
shifts.note: Capex curve steepening — Q3 capex nearly equal to all of H1

shifts.trend: RPO $523.3B (Nov 2025) → $552.6B (Feb 2026); was $97.3B a year ago (5.7x expansion)
$42.7B senior notes issued in Q3 FY26 to fund data-center buildout — capital markets execution confirming the capex commitment

ORCL.verdict: OCI at +84% with RPO at $553B is the clearest evidence of hyperscaler AI workload shift to Oracle. The capex tripling is funded (senior notes + operating cash flow). Story intact and accelerating.

PLTR.headline: Revenue re-accelerated from +53% FY25 to +85% Q1 2026; commercial outpacing government (+95% vs +76%); operating margin expanded from ~31% to 46% as contribution margins hit 66%.

shifts.note: Acceleration broad-based but commercial is the inflection point

shifts.trend: Operating margin ~31% (FY25, imputed from income $1.41B / revenue $4.5B) → 46% (Q1 2026); gross margin 80% → 87%
shifts.note: Operating leverage compounding fast

shifts.trend: Commercial revenue 46% of FY25 → 47% Q1 2026; commercial share growing on AIP bootcamp-driven enterprise adoption
US revenue 74% (FY25); government contribution margin 66% stable; AIP bootcamps the primary commercial go-to-market

PLTR.verdict: The +85% acceleration with 46% operating margin is exceptional. Commercial outpacing government signals AIP is gaining enterprise traction beyond the initial USAF/military base. The risk is whether this acceleration is durable at scale or if Q1 benefited from bootcamp timing.

PWR.headline: Backlog reached $48.47B (+10% sequentially in one quarter); RPO $26.24B; revenues +26% Q1 FY26 with operating margin expanding 50bps; data center cited as primary catalyst across both segments.

shifts.note: Sequential RPO/backlog growth of 10%+ in a single quarter is unusual for a services company

shifts.trend: Revenue +20.3% (FY25) → +26.3% (Q1 FY26); operating margin +50bps to 4.3%
Electric segment +$1.52B Q1; Underground +$117M; both segments now driven by data-center infrastructure
shifts.note: DSI (Underground) and CEI (Electric) acquisitions now generating AI-DC revenue

shifts.trend: Gross margin 14.1% (Q1 FY26) vs 13.4% (Q1 FY25) +70bps; operating income +41.7%

PWR.verdict: Sequential backlog growth of 10% in one quarter is the key signal — Quanta is the contractor capturing the multi-year buildout. The DSI and CEI acquisitions are now contributing. This is the cleanest pure-play on electrical infrastructure for data centers.

SNOW.headline: Revenue at +29-31% consistently; $1M+ customer count grew +27% to 733; RPO grew from $7.9B to $9.8B; NRR steady at 125%.

shifts.trend: Product revenue +29% (Q3 FY26) → +31% (FY26 full year) — slightly accelerating but within measurement noise
Total customers +21% (10,996 Q3 → 13,328 FY26); $1M+ customers +27% (576 → 733) — the enterprise segment growing faster than the base
RPO $7.9B (Oct 31 2025) → $9.8B (Jan 31 2026, +$1.9B/+24% in one quarter); 46-48% recognized next 12 months — near-term conversion healthy
NRR 125-126% consistent across both periods; contribution from Forbes 2000 accounts ~43% of revenue stable

SNOW.verdict: Snowflake is holding 30%+ growth with NRR 125% — a high-quality data platform. Not an AI acceleration story yet; the AI workload migration is incremental not step-change. Durable compounder but not the highest-beta AI play.

SNPS.headline: Ansys acquisition closed Jul 17, 2025; revenue +15% FY25 (including $756M Ansys), +66% Q1 FY26 (including $885M Ansys); Design IP -6% on BIS whiplash and foundry softness; $3.5B term loan repaid.

shifts.trend: Revenue +15% (FY25 with partial Ansys) → +66% (Q1 FY26 with full Ansys); Ansys $885.6M in Q1; organic growth broad except Design IP
shifts.note: Ansys growth contribution is structural — will persist

shifts.trend: China -22% ex-Ansys (FY25) from BIS is-informed letter (May 29, rescinded Jul 2); Design IP still weak in FY26 on foundry softness
$13.5B debt at FY25-end; $3.5B term loan repaid Q1 FY26; deleveraging before buyback resumes
Backlog $11.4B (YE25) → $11.3B (Jan 31 2026); Processor IP business sold to GlobalFoundries (Jan 2026) — Design IP reallocation
shifts.note: Backlog broadly flat post-Ansys — stability

shifts.trend: Design IP: 'muted growth expected FY26'; exposure to foundry customer softness and BIS lingering effects
shifts.note: Design IP is the weak spot within the SNPS portfolio

SNPS.verdict: Ansys integration is the story — $39B all-in for a multi-physics simulation monopoly that diversifies SNPS beyond EDA. Deleveraging is on track. Design IP weakness is real but contained. Thesis intact: EDA+simulation toll-booth with AI tailwinds.

VRT.headline: Americas accelerated from +41.9% FY25 to +53.1% Q1 FY26; gross margin expanded 400bps YoY to 37.7%; combined order backlog doubled to $15.0B at YE25 and appears to continue growing.

shifts.trend: Americas +41.9% (FY25) → +53.1% (Q1 FY26); total revenue +27.7% → +30.1%; consolidated operating profit +33.8% (FY25) → +51.4% (Q1 FY26)
Consolidated gross margin 36.3% (FY25, -30bps) → 37.7% (Q1 FY26, +400bps YoY) — Americas operating margin 26.8% → 27.0%
shifts.note: FY25 had tariff-driven compression; Q1 FY26 is recovering and then some

shifts.trend: Combined order backlog $7.2B (YE24) → $15.0B (YE25, +108%); majority firm and expected to ship within 12-18 months
FY26 capex guided $425-525M vs $226.4M FY25 (2x); capacity expansion for liquid cooling and thermal management products
Asia Pacific +17.5% FY25 → +12.2% Q1 FY26 (decelerating, timing-driven); EMEA flat FY25 → stable — Americas is the only accelerating geography

VRT.verdict: Americas acceleration with margin expansion is the clearest confirmation that Vertiv's liquid-cooling position is inflecting with Blackwell deployments. Backlog doubling provides 12-18 months visibility. Boyd Thermal (ETN acquisition) may introduce incremental competition, but current lead is wide.
